stories.title: Knicks Parade DAY — Canyon of Heroes Happening NOW, First Ticker-Tape in Franchise History
stories.angle: The day is finally here. The New York Knicks championship parade rolls through the Canyon of Heroes on Thursday, June 18, beginning at 10:00 AM from Battery Park — the franchise's first-ever ticker-tape parade despite winning titles in 1970 and 1973. Mayor Mamdani hosts the Key to the City ceremony at City Hall Plaza. The city applied 347,000 people for 600 lottery tickets to the City Hall ceremony. Knicks legends Walt "Clyde" Frazier and Patrick Ewing are participating. There is a 40% chance of rain, but New York isn't backing down.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
IT'S PARADE DAY, NEW YORK. 🏆

Knicks Championship Parade
TODAY | June 18 | 10:00 AM ET
Battery Park → Canyon of Heroes → City Hall

53 years in the making. The first Canyon of Heroes parade in franchise history. #Knicks #NewYorkForever

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
347,000 New Yorkers applied for 600 City Hall lottery seats.

That's how much this city wants to see Jalen Brunson get the Key to the City.

The parade starts at 10 AM. Broadway belongs to the Knicks today. 🗽 #Knicks #NewYorkForever

article.headline: Knicks Parade Day: The Canyon of Heroes Gets Its First New York Basketball Celebration

stories.title: Giannis Trade Race — Heat vs. Celtics, 5 Days to June 23 Deadline
stories.angle: The Giannis Antetokounmpo trade market is tightening. The Miami Heat and Boston Celtics have emerged as the two front-runners, with the Heat remaining the clear favorite. The Bucks have not been impressed with Miami's latest package (Tyler Herro + picks), and some teams are pressing Milwaukee to name a price they would accept. The June 23 NBA Draft is the de facto deadline — five days away. Marc Stein reports a deal is "drawing near."

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
GIANNIS TRADE UPDATE 🚨

It's down to two teams: Heat and Celtics.

Bucks haven't been impressed with Miami's offer.
Celtics face a Tatum/Brown dilemma.

5 days to June 23. This market is about to explode. #NBA #GiannisAntetokounmpo

stories.title: NBA Draft 5 Days Away — Dybantsa -450 to Wizards, Peterson Pushing
stories.angle: The 2026 NBA Draft is five days away (June 23-24, Barclays Center Brooklyn). AJ Dybantsa remains the -450 favorite for No. 1 to the Washington Wizards. Darryn Peterson is still in the conversation. Washington has narrowed its focus to the two players — both visited the Wizards, Dybantsa to Wizards + Jazz. Dybantsa's stats: 25.5 PPG/51.0% FG/6.8 RPG — first player since Larry Bird (1978-79) to average those numbers and earn consensus All-American.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
AJ Dybantsa this season at BYU:
25.5 PPG | 51.0% FG | 6.8 RPG

The last player to hit those numbers AND earn consensus All-American?
Larry Bird. Indiana State. 1978-79.

5 days until the 2026 NBA Draft. 🏀 #NBADraft #Dybantsa

stories.title: Austin Reaves — Lakers the Favorites to Retain as Nets Reportedly Back Off
stories.angle: The Brooklyn Nets may not be the aggressive suitors for Austin Reaves that the league expected. League executives believe the Lakers remain the favorites to retain Reaves, who holds a $14.9M player option expiring June 29. The Lakers can offer 5 years/$239M — vs. the Nets' 4-year/$177M. Reaves (23.3 PPG/49% FG, age 28) wants to stay in Los Angeles with Luka Doncic.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Austin Reaves free agency update:

Lakers offer: 5 yrs / $239M
Nets ceiling: 4 yrs / $177M

Reports: Nets may not pursue as aggressively as expected.
League execs believe Lakers are the frontrunners to keep him.

He wants to stay with Luka. 🤞 #LakersNation #NBA

stories.title: Brunson Legacy — The Greatest Knick Ever?
stories.angle: Parade day is a good time for history. After Jalen Brunson's 45-point, unanimous MVP Game 5 performance to clinch the first Knicks title since 1973, the debate is real: is Brunson the greatest Knick ever? He took a $113M pay cut to build a champion, averaged 32.6 pts/4.6 ast in the 2026 Finals, and delivered the most iconic individual performance in franchise history. The case for Brunson over Walt "Clyde" Frazier and Willis Reed deserves a fair hearing.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
On parade day, it's worth saying out loud:

Jalen Brunson is the greatest Knick of all time.

45 points in Game 5.
$113M pay cut to build a champion.
First title since 1973.

The debate is over. #Knicks #JalenBrunson #NewYorkForever

article.headline: On Parade Day, the Greatest Knick Ever Debate Has a Clear Answer
